checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?
Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?
Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?
Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: No 'Inventory' or 'CostOfGoodsSold' concept present in the provided dataset for CASE_09

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.location: Income Statement - Quarterly and Annual Revenue

evidence.quote: InterestExpense=32165807.65 (FY2013) vs InterestExpense=19629651.82 (FY2012), a 64% jump, yet NetIncomeLoss=53966370.17 (FY2013) remained close to NetIncomeLoss=46110463.48 (FY2012)
evidence.location: Income Statement - Interest Expense & Net Income

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?
Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 10-K filed 2011-02-25 (accession 0001144204-11-011064) reports fiscal year-end data as of 2009-12-31 - filed roughly 14 months after that fiscal year-end
evidence.location: Filing metadata vs period end date

evidence.quote: 10-K filed 2012-02-29 (accession 0001144204-12-011985) reports fiscal year-end data as of 2010-12-31 - again ~14 months after fiscal year-end
evidence.location: Filing metadata vs period end date

evidence.quote: 10-K filed 2013-02-28 (accession 0001193125-13-084387) reports fiscal year-end data as of 2011-12-31 - ~14 months after fiscal year-end
evidence.location: Filing metadata vs period end date

evidence.quote: Single 10-K filed 2014-02-27 (accession 0001193125-14-072954) reports both 2012-12-31 and 2013-12-31 fiscal year-end data together, suggesting the FY2012 annual report was not separately/timely filed
evidence.location: Filing metadata vs period end dates

evidence.quote: Multiple 10-K/A amendments filed annually: 10-K/A filing_date 2010-04-30, 10-K/A filing_date 2011-04-29, 10-K/A filing_date 2012-04-27, 10-K/A filing_date 2013-04-30

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Quarterly and annual data available for Revenues, NetIncomeLoss, OperatingCashFlow, Assets, Liabilities, AccountsReceivable, Goodwill and Intangibles spanning FY2008-FY2013
No Inventory, COGS, or allowance-for-doubtful-accounts concepts provided

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Chronic ~12-14 month delay in filing annual 10-Ks (e.g., FY2009 10-K filed 2011-02-25, FY2010 10-K filed 2012-02-29, FY2011 10-K filed 2013-02-28, and FY2012/FY2013 combined into a single 10-K filed 2014-02-27) is consistent with unresolved internal control weaknesses, purchase-accounting complexity from serial acquisitions, or restatement activity that delayed certification of prior-period financials, raising the risk that reported asset/liability balances for the delayed periods required correction before final filing.

rationale_evidence.quote: 10-K filed 2011-02-25 (accession 0001144204-11-011064) shows period end 2009-12-31 as the latest annual balance sheet date
rationale_evidence.location: Filing metadata vs period end date

rationale_evidence.quote: 10-K filed 2014-02-27 (accession 0001193125-14-072954) covers both 2012-12-31 and 2013-12-31 annual balance sheet dates in one filing
rationale_evidence.location: Filing metadata vs period end date

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Intangible assets and goodwill continued to grow steadily (Goodwill=94.10M at 2011-12-31 to 95.12M at 2012-12-31 to 97.35M at 2013-12-31; IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill=653.67M at 2011-12-31 to 745.77M at 2012-12-31 to 824.21M at 2013-12-31) even though consolidated Revenues declined in FY2012 (Revenues=149,118,089.77 vs 155,872,736.58 in FY2011). No impairment charge is evident in the data, suggesting goodwill/intangible carrying values may not have been adequately tested/written down for the FY2012 revenue contraction, potentially overstating non-current assets and net income.

Quarterly net income and operating income show unusually low quarter-to-quarter variance (e.g., FY2012 quarterly NetIncomeLoss of 11.63M, 12.05M, 11.43M, 11.00M) despite meaningfully volatile top-line results (FY2012 revenue declined year-over-year and FY2013 interest expense rose 64% to 32.17M from 19.63M), which could indicate use of discretionary accruals/expense timing to smooth reported earnings around acquisition and financing activity.

top_signals: Chronic ~12-14 month delay in annual 10-K filings each year from FY2009 through FY2013, culminating in two fiscal years (FY2012 and FY2013) being reported in a single 10-K filed 2014-02-27, alongside multiple 10-K/A and 10-Q/A amendments in most years
Intangible assets and goodwill grew every period (Intangibles 653.67M→745.77M→824.21M FY2011-FY2013) with no evident impairment despite a revenue decline in FY2012 (155.87M→149.12M)

Unusually smooth quarterly net income (e.g., FY2012 quarters within a ~1M range) despite volatile revenue and a 64% jump in FY2013 interest expense, alongside receivables growing faster than a declining revenue base in FY2012
